lessons.title: Why Indications Form: Appearance, Timing, and Persistence
objectives: Relate discontinuities inherent in various materials to the indications they produce
Predict the appearance, time to appear, and persistence of indications from different discontinuity types

lessons.title: Environment, Smearing, Sequence, and Part Preparation
objectives: Explain the effects of temperature and lighting (white to ultraviolet) on indication visibility
Describe how metal smearing operations such as shot peening and machining mask discontinuities
State the preferred inspection sequence and part preparation requirements including stripping
cp105Topics: 3.1.6 Effects of temperature and lighting [white to ultraviolet (UV)]
3.1.7 Effects of metal smearing operations (shot peening, machining, etc.)

lessons.title: Factors Affecting Indications
objectives: Analyze how precleaning quality, penetrant selection, prior processing, and technique affect indications
Diagnose weak or missing indications back to their causal factor
